hallucinations.description: The paper cites works that are not substantiated by the provided project materials and are likely nonexistent or unverifiable in the claimed form. In particular, the citation 'Mehlman & Narayanan, 2025' appears suspicious: it is presented as published work in 2025 and used to support robustness claims, but no such source is established anywhere in the codebase or task materials. The paper also cites 'Mehta et al., 2022' with a title that is not clearly standard and may be fabricated or misattributed. These citations are used to frame prior work despite lacking verifiable grounding in the supplied materials.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper: "Recent work shows that pre-trained representations can improve worst-group accuracy (Mehta et al., 2022), and that SSL features exhibit different robustness properties than supervised features (Mehlman & Narayanan, 2025)." References: "Nick Mehlman and Shri Narayanan. Adversarial robustness of self-supervised learning features. IEEE Open Journal of Signal Processing, 6:468–477, 2025."

hallucinations.description: The paper claims a systematic comparison between SimCLR and MAE with extensive ablations on architectural components, normalization, decoder complexity, masking ratio, and augmentation stochasticity, but the main experimental code corresponding to the best solution only compares SimCLR and MAE on three datasets and does not implement these ablations. The paper presents these ablations as part of the study rather than as separate exploratory runs, overstating what the actual main method executed in the supplied code does.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper: "We conduct systematic ablations on: data augmentation presence, projection head architecture, feature normalization, embedding dimensionality (32, 64, 128, 256), temperature scaling (0.5 vs 1.0), MAE decoder complexity, MAE masking ratio (0%, 50%, 75%), pooling strategy, and augmentation stochasticity." Main code in best_solution_06d3877e417d4a3e84dbffee9f553746.py only trains SimCLR and MAE once per dataset with fixed settings; no such ablation loops are present.

hallucinations.description: Several reported numerical results in the paper do not match the supplied code outputs. The paper claims both methods completely fail on Color-MNIST with 0% worst-group accuracy, but the provided ablation summary and code outputs include multiple runs where MAE achieves non-zero worst-group accuracy on Color-MNIST (e.g., 0.0925, 0.0275, 0.1075 depending on variant/seed). Thus the paper overstates the result as complete failure for MAE. Likewise, the exact headline numbers for CIFAR10-BG and Fashion-Pattern do not align with the main summarized code results.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper abstract/results: "Both methods completely fail on color-based spurious correlations (0% worst-group accuracy)." But code summaries include: ablation_summary.json -> "Color-MNIST MAE ... Worst-Group Test Accuracy ... final_value: 0.0925"; another run: "Color-MNIST MAE (Original) ... final_value: 0.0275"; patch-MAE run: "Color-MNIST MAE Patch-75% ... final_value: 0.1075".

hallucinations.description: The paper's central quantitative claims for SimCLR vs MAE on CIFAR10-BG and Fashion-Pattern do not faithfully reflect the supplied experiment outputs. For example, the paper reports SimCLR 24.0% vs MAE 5.2% on CIFAR10-BG and SimCLR 68.0% vs MAE 38.8% on Fashion-Pattern, but the research summary and multiple experiment logs show different values, including CIFAR10-BG MAE 5.25% but SimCLR 24.0% paired with Fashion-Pattern MAE 38.75% only in some summaries, while the main best-node runs report other values such as Fashion-Pattern SimCLR 0.4925 or 0.705 depending on seed/setup. The paper presents these as definitive results from 'the study' despite the provided codebase containing inconsistent outputs across runs and seeds, with no single executed main script clearly producing the exact paper table/figure values as reported.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper: "MAE exhibits substantially worse robustness ... achieving worst-group accuracies of 5.2% and 38.8% compared to SimCLR’s 24.0% and 68.0% on CIFAR10-BG and Fashion-Pattern respectively." But research_summary.json reports: "CIFAR10-BG (SimCLR) 0.24, (MAE) 0.0525; Fashion-Pattern (SimCLR) 0.68, (MAE) 0.3875" while seed runs in research_summary show Fashion-Pattern SimCLR values such as 0.4925, 0.6675, 0.705.

hallucinations.description: The paper defines the Spurious Feature Reliance Score (SFRS) as (acc_avg - acc_worst) / acc_avg and states lower is better. However, the text then reports SFRS values inconsistent with the reported accuracies. For Fashion-Pattern, using the paper's own numbers for SimCLR (avg not explicitly given there, but from the code-style setup and stated group behavior) does not support the exact SFRS values quoted. More directly, for CIFAR10-BG the paper claims SimCLR SFRS = 0.519 and MAE = 0.896, but the supplied metrics corresponding to similar accuracies yield different values in code outputs (e.g., 0.4809 and 0.9261). The paper presents exact computed values that do not consistently match the formula when checked against available experiment outputs.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper: "SFRS metric quantifies this: SimCLR achieves 0.519 and 0.139 on CIFAR10-BG and Fashion-Pattern respectively, compared to MAE’s 0.896 and 0.437." Code metrics in ablation_summary.json for corresponding baseline-style setup: "CIFAR10-BG SimCLR ... SFRS ... 0.4809" and "CIFAR10-BG MAE ... 0.9261"; "Fashion-Pattern SimCLR ... 0.2803" and "Fashion-Pattern MAE ... 0.2962".

hallucinations.description: The paper claims to implement MAE as 'masked image modeling' by masking random patches and reconstructing only masked content, but the main supplied MAE implementation in the core experiment code reconstructs the full image with plain MSE and applies no masking at all. A separate ablation later explores patch masking, but the main method description incorrectly states patch masking as the base MAE training procedure.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper method section: "For MAE (He et al., 2021), we mask random patches and train the model to reconstruct the original image using MSE loss." Main code in best_solution_06d3877e417d4a3e84dbffee9f553746.py: "recon = mae_model(imgs); loss = F.mse_loss(recon, imgs)" with no patch masking logic.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found. The paper overstates and misreports both methodology and results relative to the supplied code, especially around MAE masking behavior, extensive ablations, and exact quantitative outcomes. There are also likely unreliable or nonexistent citations. Overall severity is moderate to high because the core empirical claims and parts of the method description are not faithfully grounded in the provided implementation.
